frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Girls, I am still shaking after my 1:1 today — my manager (a WOMAN, mind you) literally told me I need to 'put more effort into my appearance' for client meetings 🙃 Like excuse me, I shower, I dress professionally, but apparently no kajal = not presentable enough?? And the same corporate culture will turn around and call women who wear full makeup 'trying too hard' or 'not serious' — we literally cannot win either way and I'm so tired of my face being part of my performance review.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: Priya is articulating and critiquing the double bind women face regarding appearance standards in professional settings — this is feminist consciousness-raising, not misogynistic expression. The message encodes frustration at a gendered institutional norm rather than policing or demeaning women.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Pune. You have been told by your female manager informally that you should make more effort with your appearance because client facing roles require a certain standard of presentation. You have never worn much makeup. You are posting anonymously in a women only WhatsApp group after this conversation sharing what your manager said and how it made you feel and the impossible position women are put in Ñ judged for wearing too much and judged for wearing too little.
